<link="https://www.uvic.ca/science/assets/docs/deans_lists/DeansList2016-17.pdf"><style="Link">UVic Dean’s List (2016/17)</style></link>
Recognizes top 10% of students in the Faculty of Science according to their year.
<style="Title">Thoughts on AI</style>

I completed my post-secondary education before the mainstream adoption of large language models such as ChatGPT. As a result, I developed a strong foundation in computer science and mathematics, and gained early professional experience without relying on AI-assisted tools.

I view modern AI as highly capable and broadly useful, especially within software development. However, its effectiveness depends on thoughtful application rather than overreliance. Quality work still requires judgment, problem-solving, and accountability.

As AI continues to shape the role of developers, I actively incorporate it where it adds value while maintaining ownership of outcomes. I approach it as a tool to enhance productivity and insight, not a substitute for critical thinking.
<style="Title">Who am I?</style>

I'm proud to be a follower of Jesus Christ. He has made me a better man in every aspect of life. Though I'm far from perfect, I'm striving to be more like him. That means I'm a hard worker and an honest worker. When life gets tough, I keep showing up because my life is built on a solid foundation.

I'm happily married to my highschool sweetheart who is both beautiful and brilliant and pushes me to be a better man. We met as part of our school's student council, and we're on this adventure of life together.
Oh, and fair warning, I like to laugh a lot, so if you spend too much time with me, you may be in danger of thinking you are funnier than you really are.
<style="Title">Hobbies & Extracurriculars</style>

Wow, you made it this far? Impressive.
Some of my hobbies include:
<style="Bullet">Shoveling snow (living in Saskatoon SK for my wife's schooling)</style><style="Bullet">Playing and making video games (this website is meant as a learning tool)</style><style="Bullet">Bouldering (i.e. climbing plastic rocks, and occasional real ones)</style><style="Bullet">Calisthenics because I'm too cheap to lift weights (currently working on handstands)</style><style="Bullet">Volunteering as a youth leader (makes me feel old, but keeps me up to date with what the kids are doing)</style><style="Bullet">Not a hobby, but I was part of the crew on a 70ft long, concrete hulled sail boat that circumnavigated Vancouver Island when I was 16 years old. It was quite the adventure.</style><style="Bullet">In the future I'd like to learn archery (compound bows), get more experience with firearms, and get into backpacking/bushcraft.</style>
Cloud Software Developer
AXYS Technologies Inc. Victoria, BC. May 2022 – Present

- Full stack development of a data standardization pipeline with an API and front-end management portal.
        - Implemented with ASP.NET Core w/ Telerik, Python, FastApi, SqlAlchemy, Alembic, and Redis among others.
        - Provisioned with IaC using AWS CDK for Python.
    - Engaged in system design, tool exploration, code reviews and agile processes such as stakeholder reviews.
Teaching Assistant
University of Victoria. Victoria, BC. Sep. 2019 – Apr. 2022

- Taught students the fundamentals of programming in C as a lab instructor and marker.
    - Marked and gave feedback on assignments for Algorithms and Data Structures course.
    - Taught business students the fundamentals of computing (topics included SQL, JavaScript, CSS, and HTML).
Software Developer (Co-op)
National Research Council of Canada: Herzberg Astronomy and Astrophysics Research Centre (Software Group) Victoria, BC. Jan. 2021 – Sep. 2021

- Worked in a team of seven to design, develop, and test HEART, a toolkit written in C that facilitates the development of real time control systems for adaptive optics in large telescopes across the world.
    - Contributed to a large project with many moving parts requiring concurrent programming, careful memory management, and object-oriented programming.
Web Application Developer (Co-op)
BC Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction: Research Branch, Victoria, BC. May. 2020 – Aug. 2020

- Built a web application for data visualization using React and implemented a full test suite with Jest.
    - Performed full stack development, continuous code reviews and agile development processes.
Quality Assurance Analyst (Co-op)
Latitude Geographics (VertiGIS North America). Victoria, BC. May. 2019 – Aug. 2019

- Performed regression and manual feature testing as well as developed an automated testing suite.
    - Communicated frequently with developer and support teams to ensure top product quality.
Data Analyst (Co-op)
Slegg Building Materials. Victoria, BC. May. 2018 – Aug. 2018
- Summarized large reports on customers, inventory, and sales.
Student Painting Branch Manager
Scholars Edge Painting. Victoria, BC. Oct. 2016 – Sep. 2017

- Sought for, interviewed, hired, trained, and coordinated employees on a tight schedule.
    - Sold and produced over $77,000 worth of painting contracts from May to August of 2017.
